target.title: discord/picker: alpha-bucket select for >25 providers or models
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- `provider/*` wildcard configs frequently expand to 50+ models for a single provider; the Discord model picker rendered them as long prev/next paginated dropdowns that were tedious to scan.

Why does this matter now?

- The wildcard rollout is making large-provider lists the common case (vLLM auto-discovery in particular), and the existing picker UX assumed sub-25-item lists.

What is the intended outcome?

- Provider selection always renders as a dropdown with pagination instead of provider buttons. When a provider or model list exceeds Discord's 25-option select cap, a letter-range bucket select appears above the relevant dropdown (`A–G (12) · H–N (18) · O–Z (5)`).

What is intentionally out of scope?

- The wildcard-load defer fix (event-loop blocking from unbounded catalog load) ships as a separate PR — it is independent of this UX change and reviewable on its own merits.
- Telegram / Slack / Mattermost / WhatsApp pickers — Telegram has the same strict-equality landmines on a different code path; Slack and Mattermost have no picker yet. Each warrants its own change.

What does success look like?

- Users with 100+ models for one provider can jump straight to the right letter range with one click instead of paging through 4–5 prev/next clicks.
- No regression for users whose configs stay under 25 items.

What should reviewers focus on?

- Discord component limits: providers now render as one string-select row with pagination, not a provider button grid, so the picker stays under both the 5-row and 25-option limits (`model-picker.state.ts`, `model-picker.view.ts`).
- customId encoding: bucket state is derived at re-render via `findProviderBucketId` / `findModelBucketId` where possible, while provider select and nav customIds carry only the state needed to preserve the active page/bucket.

## Linked context

Closes #

Related #

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

- No — author-initiated UX improvement.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Discord `/models` and `/model` pickers need to stay usable when wildcard providers expose large provider/model catalogs. Provider selection now stays in a dropdown with pagination, and model/provider bucket selectors keep the visible options under Discord's 25-option select cap.
- Real environment tested: local vitest against the PR worktree plus a rebuilt Docker dev gateway running the Discord plugin with the Dev bot in a real Discord guild.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=30000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-discord.config.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/model-picker.test.ts`
  - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=30000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-discord.config.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/native-command.model-picker.test.ts`
  - `docker compose build openclaw-gateway`
  - `docker compose up -d --force-recreate openclaw-gateway`
  - `curl -sS http://127.0.0.1:19250/healthz`
  - Manual Discord `/model` invocation against the dev bot.
- Evidence after fix:
  - `model-picker.test.ts`: `Test Files 1 passed (1) / Tests 39 passed (39)`
  - `native-command.model-picker.test.ts`: `Test Files 1 passed (1) / Tests 18 passed (18)`
  - Docker dev gateway health: `{"ok":true,"status":"live"}`
  - Gateway logs show the Discord plugin loaded and the bot initialized as `@DevMan` in guild `1478433853671739434`.
  - Live Discord screenshot: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rendrag-git/openclaw/pr-assets-86181/pr-assets/86181/live-discord-model-picker.png
- Observed result after fix: the live Discord picker renders the bucket dropdown in the model picker, and the provider view renders providers through a dropdown instead of provider buttons while preserving pagination/bucket state.
- What was not tested: automated CI cannot exercise the live Discord guild/bot. The live Discord proof is manual dev-gateway smoke evidence; the serialized Discord component payloads and interaction handler paths are covered by focused tests.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: the dev gateway is a Docker-local environment on port 19250 using the configured Discord Dev bot token; it is not a packaged release install.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): the original branch used provider buttons and had a model-bucketing path where the provider selector disappeared with no route back to provider selection.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/discord/src/monitor/model-picker.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/native-command.model-picker.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/model-picker-preferences.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs`
- `/home/ubuntu/projects/codexcustomization/reviewloop/bin/reviewloop -- --base main` (3 passes; findings from each pass closed and pinned with regression tests)

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- `computeAlphaBuckets`: single-bucket below threshold, letter ranges for diverse input, no stray-letter spillover, numeric fallback when prefixes collide, empty input, bucket count ≤ 25 for 501- and 5000-item lists.
- `getDiscordModelPickerProviderPage` / `getDiscordModelPickerModelPage`: bucket-aware paging, single-page fits within bucketed row budget.
- Provider view renders a provider select rather than provider buttons; provider select and nav rows preserve active bucket/page state.
- Model select customId asserts length ≤ 100 chars for long provider + snowflake user id with full bucket state.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- N/A — this is the introducing PR. Earlier branch commits pinned three reviewloop-surfaced regressions (customId overflow, row-budget overrun, model-pagination state drop) that are now closed.

If no test was added, why not?

- All findings have regression coverage.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes`)

- Yes — Discord `/models` and `/model` pickers use dropdown-based provider selection, with letter-range bucket selects when provider or model count exceeds 25.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`No`)

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`No`)

What is the highest-risk area?

- customId state preservation across the picker's interaction graph. Provider/model buckets and pages must survive dropdown selection, pagination, and the Providers back path.

How is that risk mitigated?

- Regression tests pin provider-select rendering, bucket/page preservation, and customId length ≤ 100. Manual round-trip testing against the live Discord dev bot now verifies the bucket picker appears in a real guild.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- Maintainer review of the dropdown provider selection, bucket-state derivation contract (`findProviderBucketId` / `findModelBucketId`), and live Discord proof.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- CI and maintainer review. Live Discord interaction proof is now attached as a screenshot from the dev gateway.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- Reviewloop pass 1: dispatch/persist double-write race in apply path — addressed by a separate PR (out of scope here).
- Reviewloop pass 2: bucket count > 25 for huge same-prefix lists; modelBucket round-trip on model select — addressed in 4fdeca0.
- Reviewloop pass 3: customId length > 100 for long provider + 20-digit user id; row budget overrun with bucket select active; providerBucket dropped on model pagination — addressed in a4960776.

target.title: [Feature]: Channel Broker Phase 1 protocol foundation
target.body:
## Maintainer TL;DR This stack consolidates the recurring Telegram/Discord/Slack/WhatsApp/Signal/iMessage/etc. maintenance churn behind one Channel Broker contract. OpenClaw keeps the shared semantics that repeatedly regress across native channel plugins: sessions, allowlists, routing, /verbose, streaming policy, durable final sends, receipts, retries, and auditability. Broker providers own platform mechanics: bot APIs, bridge/device state, native ids, delivery quirks, and provider capabilities. This PR is the narrow foundation. It creates the versioned broker SDK subpath and bundled channel-broker plugin, but it does not move Telegram, Discord, Slack, or any user defaults. It also carries a test-only agent-support mock refresh for the current upstream CI shard that was already failing on raw upstream/main. The direction is intentionally incremental: first land the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, then prove conformance, then prove opt-in routing for the major channels, then model long-tail and constrained providers. Only after migration paths exist should legacy native-channel feature work freeze in #86115. ## Why This Is Better Today, one fix to streaming, /verbose, or delivery behavior can make Telegram fast this week and then break Discord, Slack, or another native plugin the next week. The broker path makes those shared semantics one OpenClaw-owned contract instead of many platform-specific re-implementations. When a provider adds support for previews, threads, receipts, media, or constrained account modes, that capability can become available through the same contract to routed channels whose provider declares and passes conformance for it. ## Stack Shape ```mermaid flowchart TD U["Umbrella issue #86105: reduce maintained-channel churn"] --> P1["#86096 Phase 1: SDK + bundled broker foundation"] P1 --> P2A["#86153 Phase 2A: conformance + signed inbound"] P2A --> P2B["#86154 Phase 2B: Telegram opt-in proof"] P2B --> P2C["#86156 Phase 2C: Discord opt-in proof"] P2C --> P2D["#86157 Phase 2D: Slack opt-in proof"] P2D --> P3["#86164 Phase 3: official/app capability matrix"] P3 --> P4["#86165 Phase 4: constrained provider capabilities"] P4 -. "future, not in this stack" .-> P5["Future freeze issue #86115: legacy native-plugin freeze"] ``` | PR | Role in the consolidation | | --- | --- | | #86096 | Creates the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, provider routing guard, media final-send mapping, SecretRef/cancellation handling, and prefix-ownership contract; carries a test-only upstream CI mock refresh. | | #86153 | Proves shared lifecycle conformance and adds signed inbound webhook registration/runtime handoff: inbound dedupe, durable final sends, receipts, retryable failures, preview/progress gates, and /verbose channelData. | | #86154 | Proves Telegram topics can route through broker targets without native Telegram internals. | | #86156 | Proves Discord DMs/channels/threads preserve conversation semantics through broker routing. | | #86157 | Proves Slack DMs/channel threads preserve direct/thread semantics through broker routing. | | #86164 | Adds official/app platform aliases, capability matrix rows, and declared delivery capability enforcement for long-tail channels. | | #86165 | Adds constrained-provider metadata for WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and bridge-backed providers. | ## Ownership Model ```mermaid flowchart LR Core["OpenClaw core
sessions, routing, allowlists
/verbose, streaming, retries
receipts, audit"] --> Broker["channel-broker plugin
openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-broker
V1 protocol"] Broker --> Provider["Provider-owned broker
HTTP/webhook transport
platform adapters"] Provider --> Platforms["Telegram, Discord, Slack
Teams, Matrix, WhatsApp
Signal, iMessage, long tail"] Provider --> Broker Broker --> Core ``` ## Review Shape These PRs are intentionally linear. GitHub still shows `main` as the base for each PR so maintainers can land them one by one from fork branches; the branches themselves are stacked, and maintainers can review the focused adjacent diffs here: | PR | Focused adjacent diff | | --- | --- | | #86153 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-protocol...feat/channel-broker-conformance | | #86154 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-conformance...feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing | | #86156 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing...feat/channel-broker-discord-routing | | #86157 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-discord-routing...feat/channel-broker-slack-routing | | #86164 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-slack-routing...feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities | | #86165 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities...feat/channel-broker-constrained-platform-capabilities | ## This PR - Phase: Phase 1 - SDK and bundled broker foundation - Role: Creates the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, provider routing guard, media final-send mapping, SecretRef/cancellation handling, and prefix-ownership contract; includes a test-only upstream CI mock refresh. - Head: `7eb8166b1a` ## Current Review State This PR is live for maintainer review. Current head: `7eb8166b1a`. Focused local validation for this phase is documented below. Broad validation remains owned by GitHub Actions. Native channel defaults remain unchanged unless this phase explicitly says otherwise. ## Summary Adds Phase 1 of the Channel Broker consolidation program: a versioned `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-broker` protocol surface, a bundled `channel-broker` channel plugin, provider-owned outbound request/receipt helpers, docs, and focused tests. This PR intentionally stays at the protocol/foundation layer. Telegram, Discord, Slack, and later channel migrations are tracked separately and should not be folded into this PR. ## Linked Context Closes #86107 Related #86105 Related #86108 Related #86109 Related #86110 Related #86111 Related #86112 Related #86113 Related #86114 Related #86115 ## Review Repair History The current Phase 1 branch includes focused repairs for ClawSweeper's blocker classes: - Preserves native prefix ownership when `channel-broker` declares platform aliases that overlap native plugins. If Telegram/Discord/Slack are registered, their prefixes stay native-owned; broker aliases own a platform prefix only when no native plugin declares it. - Adds the standard channel SecretRef contract for `channels.channel-broker.{outboundToken,signingSecret}` plus `accounts.*` and `providers.*` variants, and changes the broker schema/docs to use SecretRef-compatible credential input. - Threads `AbortSignal` through broker outbound requests, including the HTTP `fetch` transport and custom runtime override path. ## Real Behavior Proof - **Behavior or issue addressed:** Verified the Phase 1 broker surface resolves as a bundled channel and produces a normalized V1 outbound request for a Telegram-scoped broker target. This proves the user-visible foundation being shipped in this PR: catalog discovery, channel id normalization, target construction, protocol versioning, platform normalization, thread preservation, and final-send payload shape. - **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`, branch `feat/channel-broker-protocol`, after the current Phase 1 repair/doc series through `d39d47b3db`. - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** ```bash node --import tsx -e 'const { listBundledChannelCatalogEntries } = await import("./src/channels/bundled-channel-catalog-read.ts"); const { normalizeChatChannelId } = await import("./src/channels/ids.ts"); const { buildBrokerConversationTarget, createBrokerOutboundRequest } = await import("./src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.ts"); const catalog = listBundledChannelCatalogEntries().find((entry) => entry.id === "channel-broker"); const target = buildBrokerConversationTarget({ platform: "Telegram", conversationId: "chat 123", threadId: "topic-7" }); const request = createBrokerOutboundRequest({ requestId: "send-proof-1", providerId: "synthetic-provider", platform: "Telegram", accountId: "default", conversation: { id: "chat 123", type: "channel", threadId: "topic-7" }, mode: "final", payloads: [{ text: "broker proof" }], requirements: { text: true, thread: true } }); console.log(JSON.stringify({ discoveredChannel: catalog && { id: catalog.id, order: catalog.order, docsPath: catalog.channel.docsPath }, channelNormalization: normalizeChatChannelId("channel-broker"), target, outboundProtocolVersion: request.version, outboundMode: request.mode, outboundPlatform: request.platform, outboundConversation: request.conversation, outboundPayloadText: request.payloads[0]?.text }, null, 2));' ``` - **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output copied from the command above: ```json { "discoveredChannel": { "id": "channel-broker", "order": 60, "docsPath": "/channels/channel-broker" }, "channelNormalization": "channel-broker", "target": "telegram:chat%20123?threadId=topic-7", "outboundProtocolVersion": 1, "outboundMode": "final", "outboundPlatform": "telegram", "outboundConversation": { "id": "chat 123", "type": "channel", "threadId": "topic-7" }, "outboundPayloadText": "broker proof" } ``` - **Observed result after fix:** OpenClaw discovered the bundled `channel-broker` entry, normalized the channel id, built a Telegram broker target that preserved `threadId`, and emitted a V1 final outbound request with normalized `platform: "telegram"`, `conversation.threadId: "topic-7"`, and payload text `broker proof`. - **What was not tested:** No real third-party broker provider delivery was tested in Phase 1. That is intentionally deferred to the Phase 2 migration proof issues for Telegram, Discord, and Slack. ## Focused Local Validation All local validation below ran from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`: ```bash git diff --check ``` ```bash node node_modules/oxfmt/bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 --config .oxfmtrc.jsonc docs/channels/channel-broker.md docs/reference/secretref-credential-surface.md extensions/channel-broker/src/accounts.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/outbound.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/runtime.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/secret-contract.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/secret-contract.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/types.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-target-prefix.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-target-prefix.test.ts ``` ```bash pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/secret-contract.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts ``` ```bash pnpm exec vitest run src/infra/outbound/channel-target-prefix.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.config.ts ``` ```bash pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json ``` ```bash pnpm exec vitest run test/scripts/bundled-plugin-build-entries.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.config.ts ``` ## Known Inherited Gates - `pnpm plugin-sdk:surface:check` has an inherited clean-upstream SDK budget drift; this PR adds the intended `plugin-sdk/channel-broker` public subpath. - `pnpm docs:check-i18n-glossary` has inherited unrelated glossary-label failures on clean upstream; Channel Broker glossary entries were added in this branch.

target.title: fix(agent-job): surface pending error snapshot when outer timeout fires
target.body:
### What

Fixes a race condition in `waitForAgentJob` that caused pending agent errors to be discarded when the primary timeout fired.

### Why

When an agent job fails, an `error` event schedules the job to finish with an error snapshot after a 15-second grace period. If the outer `timeoutMs` for `waitForAgentJob` fires before this grace period elapses, it calls `finish(null)`, canceling the pending error.

The caller receives a generic timeout message instead of the actual root cause, making debugging difficult.

### How

The outer timeout callback now checks for a pending error via `getPendingAgentRunError()` before resolving with `null`. This function reads from a module-level map that is populated as soon as the `error` event is received, guaranteeing that the snapshot is available if the race occurs.

A new regression test using fake timers (`5s < 15s`) confirms the fix.

Closes #52497

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Race condition in `waitForAgentJob` — outer timeout fires `finish(null)`, discarding the pending error snapshot. Caller gets a generic timeout instead of the actual agent failure reason.

**Real environment tested:** OpenClaw development clone, Node 24, Linux (WSL2). Gateway `waitForAgentJob` path exercised directly with controlled timing.

```
pnpm test src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts --reporter=verbose

**Observed result:** All 61 tests pass. The new regression test (`surfaces pending error snapshot when outer timeout fires before error grace period`) passed and covers the pending-error-snapshot path when the outer timeout fires before the error grace period.

**What was not tested:** Race with sub-100ms timeouts; concurrent multi-job error races; production OpenClaw load; error snapshots arriving after the outer timeout resolves.

target.title: [Feature]: Channel Broker Phase 2D Slack migration proof
target.body:
## Maintainer TL;DR This stack consolidates the recurring Telegram/Discord/Slack/WhatsApp/Signal/iMessage/etc. maintenance churn behind one Channel Broker contract. OpenClaw keeps the shared semantics that repeatedly regress across native channel plugins: sessions, allowlists, routing, /verbose, streaming policy, durable final sends, receipts, retries, and auditability. Broker providers own platform mechanics: bot APIs, bridge/device state, native ids, delivery quirks, and provider capabilities. This PR proves Slack DMs and channel threads can route through broker-owned semantics while native Slack remains the default path. The direction is intentionally incremental: first land the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, then prove conformance, then prove opt-in routing for the major channels, then model long-tail and constrained providers. Only after migration paths exist should legacy native-channel feature work freeze in #86115. ## Why This Is Better Today, one fix to streaming, /verbose, or delivery behavior can make Telegram fast this week and then break Discord, Slack, or another native plugin the next week. The broker path makes those shared semantics one OpenClaw-owned contract instead of many platform-specific re-implementations. When a provider adds support for previews, threads, receipts, media, or constrained account modes, that capability can become available through the same contract to routed channels whose provider declares and passes conformance for it. ## Stack Shape ```mermaid flowchart TD U["Umbrella issue #86105: reduce maintained-channel churn"] --> P1["#86096 Phase 1: SDK + bundled broker foundation"] P1 --> P2A["#86153 Phase 2A: conformance + signed inbound"] P2A --> P2B["#86154 Phase 2B: Telegram opt-in proof"] P2B --> P2C["#86156 Phase 2C: Discord opt-in proof"] P2C --> P2D["#86157 Phase 2D: Slack opt-in proof"] P2D --> P3["#86164 Phase 3: official/app capability matrix"] P3 --> P4["#86165 Phase 4: constrained provider capabilities"] P4 -. "future, not in this stack" .-> P5["Future freeze issue #86115: legacy native-plugin freeze"] ``` | PR | Role in the consolidation | | --- | --- | | #86096 | Creates the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, provider routing guard, media final-send mapping, SecretRef/cancellation handling, and prefix-ownership contract; carries a test-only upstream CI mock refresh. | | #86153 | Proves shared lifecycle conformance and adds signed inbound webhook registration/runtime handoff: inbound dedupe, durable final sends, receipts, retryable failures, preview/progress gates, and /verbose channelData. | | #86154 | Proves Telegram topics can route through broker targets without native Telegram internals. | | #86156 | Proves Discord DMs/channels/threads preserve conversation semantics through broker routing. | | #86157 | Proves Slack DMs/channel threads preserve direct/thread semantics through broker routing. | | #86164 | Adds official/app platform aliases, capability matrix rows, and declared delivery capability enforcement for long-tail channels. | | #86165 | Adds constrained-provider metadata for WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and bridge-backed providers. | ## Ownership Model ```mermaid flowchart LR Core["OpenClaw core
sessions, routing, allowlists
/verbose, streaming, retries
receipts, audit"] --> Broker["channel-broker plugin
openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-broker
V1 protocol"] Broker --> Provider["Provider-owned broker
HTTP/webhook transport
platform adapters"] Provider --> Platforms["Telegram, Discord, Slack
Teams, Matrix, WhatsApp
Signal, iMessage, long tail"] Provider --> Broker Broker --> Core ``` ## Review Shape These PRs are intentionally linear. GitHub still shows `main` as the base for each PR so maintainers can land them one by one from fork branches; the branches themselves are stacked, and maintainers can review the focused adjacent diffs here: | PR | Focused adjacent diff | | --- | --- | | #86153 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-protocol...feat/channel-broker-conformance | | #86154 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-conformance...feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing | | #86156 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing...feat/channel-broker-discord-routing | | #86157 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-discord-routing...feat/channel-broker-slack-routing | | #86164 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-slack-routing...feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities | | #86165 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities...feat/channel-broker-constrained-platform-capabilities | ## This PR - Phase: Phase 2D - Slack opt-in broker routing proof - Role: Proves Slack DMs/channel threads preserve direct/thread semantics through broker routing. - Head: `27b7eca94c` ## Current Review State This PR is live for maintainer review. Current head: `27b7eca94c`. Focused local validation for this phase is documented below. Broad validation remains owned by GitHub Actions. Native channel defaults remain unchanged unless this phase explicitly says otherwise. ## Summary - Adds the Phase 2D Slack migration proof for the Channel Broker rollout. - Keeps native Slack default behavior unchanged; this only proves opt-in broker routing for Slack targets. - Stacks on Phase 2C Discord proof (#86156), Phase 2B Telegram proof (#86154), Phase 2A conformance baseline (#86153), and Phase 1 broker foundation (#86096). Closes #86112 Related #86108 Related #86105 Supersedes closed PR #86142, which was closed by the repository active-PR cap rather than by an implementation failure. ## What changed - Normalize broker-prefixed Slack DM targets such as `broker:slack:user:U12345678` into broker provider requests with `conversation.type = "direct"`. - Normalize broker-prefixed Slack channel thread targets such as `broker:slack:channel:C12345678?threadId=1716500000.000001` into broker provider requests with `conversation.type = "channel"` and `threadId`. - Canonicalize Slack broker session routes to the resolved platform target, preserving direct-route semantics as `slack:direct%3A` and thread routes as `slack:?threadId=`. ## Real Behavior Proof - **Behavior or issue addressed:** Slack DM and threaded-channel targets can route through `channel-broker` while preserving direct/channel conversation semantics and Slack `threadTs` as broker `threadId`. - **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`, using the Channel Broker plugin and SDK from the stacked rollout branch on May 24, 2026. - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a `node --import tsx --input-type=module` terminal proof that called `channelBrokerPlugin.message.send.text` with `to: "broker:slack:channel:C12345678?threadId=1716500000.000001"` and captured the provider request passed to the configured broker runtime. - **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output excerpt from that local command: ```json { "label": "slack-thread", "messageId": "native-slack-thread", "platform": "slack", "conversation": { "id": "C12345678", "type": "channel", "threadId": "1716500000.000001" }, "requirements": { "text": true, "thread": true } } ``` - **Observed result after fix:** The live plugin path produced the expected Slack provider request and kept threaded delivery represented by the broker `threadId` field. - **What was not tested:** Live Slack API traffic and the full local suite; this PR stays scoped to opt-in broker routing proof. Focused diff for this stack layer: https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-discord-routing...feat/channel-broker-slack-routing ## Validation Run locally from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`: - `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts` - `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json` - `git diff --check` - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/channel-broker/src/target.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts` - `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/channel-broker --format unix` ## Review state - Live for maintainer review; no longer draft. - No native Slack migration is enabled by default in this PR. - Includes a tiny test-isolation hardening for `src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts` after the unrelated `checks-node-agentic-agents` shard repeatedly timed out there while the single file passed locally.

target.title: fix(doctor): recover sibling moltbot config
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #54200 by adding a narrow recovery path for the upgrade layout where `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` is missing or skeletal while the user's real legacy config still exists at `~/.openclaw/moltbot.json`.

- `doctor` preflight copies sibling `moltbot.json` into canonical `openclaw.json` when the canonical file is missing.
- If canonical `openclaw.json` exists but is skeletal, read-only doctor now only warns; `openclaw doctor --fix` backs it up and restores from sibling `moltbot.json`.
- The replacement path copies through a temp file, restores the original canonical config on failure, and chmods the recovered canonical config to `0600`.
- Missing canonical config recovery now installs with no-overwrite semantics, so a concurrently-created canonical `openclaw.json` is preserved.
- Gateway startup adds a targeted hint when `gateway.mode` is missing and the sibling legacy config exists.

This does **not** add `moltbot.json` back to the normal config resolver or public config candidate list.

## Verification

2026-05-24 update, head `6be3f29935afda151365d42df45399357e239eaf`:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-config-preflight.test.ts src/cli/gateway-cli/run.option-collisions.test.ts --reporter=dot` -> 2 files / 36 tests passed
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/commands/doctor-config-preflight.ts src/commands/doctor-config-preflight.test.ts src/cli/gateway-cli/run.option-collisions.test.ts src/cli/gateway-cli/run.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/commands/doctor-config-preflight.ts src/commands/doctor-config-preflight.test.ts src/cli/gateway-cli/run.option-collisions.test.ts src/cli/gateway-cli/run.ts`
- `git diff --check refs/remotes/origin/main...HEAD`

Earlier verification:

- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --write --threads=1 src/commands/doctor-config-preflight.ts src/commands/doctor-config-preflight.test.ts src/cli/gateway-cli/run.ts src/cli/gateway-cli/run.option-collisions.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/commands/doctor-config-preflight.ts src/commands/doctor-config-preflight.test.ts src/cli/gateway-cli/run.ts src/cli/gateway-cli/run.option-collisions.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-config-preflight.test.ts src/cli/gateway-cli/run.option-collisions.test.ts --reporter=dot` (2 files, 35 tests passed)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/paths.test.ts src/commands/doctor-config-flow.test.ts src/commands/doctor-config-preflight.test.ts src/cli/gateway-cli/run.option-collisions.test.ts --reporter=dot` (4 files, 92 tests passed)
- `git diff --check refs/remotes/origin/main...HEAD`

`pnpm check:changed` reaches an unrelated current-main core-test typecheck failure in `scripts/control-ui-i18n.ts` for missing declarations on `./npm-runner.mjs`, `./pnpm-runner.mjs`, and `./windows-cmd-helpers.mjs`. The core production typecheck lane passed before that failure.

## Real behavior proof

Real temp home: `/tmp/openclaw-54200-proof-2ODsvV` with:

- skeletal canonical config: `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` containing only `update.channel`
- sibling legacy config: `~/.openclaw/moltbot.json` containing `gateway.mode=local`, `gateway.port=19091`, and `agents.defaults.model`

target.title: fix(config): skip shell env fallback on Windows
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #84795 by making the optional login-shell env fallback a no-op on native Windows before it can resolve or execute `/bin/sh`.

The existing POSIX behavior is unchanged. `getShellPathFromLoginShell` already skipped `win32`; this applies the same platform boundary to the direct `loadShellEnvFallback` path used during config loading.

## Verification

2026-05-24 native Windows proof from this branch, head `fde7096e11e8b21dc474d1f9ceac3ad330432e33`:

- Native Windows Node: `v22.22.3`
- Built a local smoke bundle from this branch with native Windows esbuild:
  `node_modules\@esbuild\win32-x64\esbuild.exe src\entry.ts --bundle --platform=node --format=esm --packages=external --outfile=.\oc85739-entry.mjs`
- Ran the affected startup/status path with shell-env fallback enabled:
  `$env:OPENCLAW_LOAD_SHELL_ENV='1'; $env:OPENCLAW_HOME='C:\tmp\oc85739-home'; node .\oc85739-entry.mjs status`
  - exit code: `0`
  - stderr: empty
  - stdout scan for `/bin/sh|ENOENT`: `False`
  - stdout includes `OpenClaw status` and `OS windows 10.0.26200 (x64) · node 22.22.3`
- Direct fallback proof from the same native Windows checkout also returned:
  `{ "platform": "win32", "result": { "ok": true, "applied": [] }, "warnings": [] }`

2026-05-23 update after rebasing onto `origin/main` (`acf265d4d5`), head `8ef82383f5`:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/shell-env.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (1 file, 21 tests passed)
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md src/infra/shell-env.ts src/infra/shell-env.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/infra/shell-env.ts src/infra/shell-env.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`

Earlier verification before the rebase:

- `corepack pnpm install`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md src/infra/shell-env.ts src/infra/shell-env.test.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/infra/shell-env.ts src/infra/shell-env.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- Testbox-through-Crabbox, provider `blacksmith-testbox`, id `tbx_01ksam41ew6rr7b0zmwf5hacq7`, Actions run https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26335373158:
  `CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 OPENCLAW_TESTBOX_REMOTE_RUN=1 corepack pnpm test src/infra/shell-env.test.ts`
  - Result: 1 file / 21 tests passed.
- Autoreview: `/mnt/c/src/claws-hapi/.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`
  - Result: clean, no accepted/actionable findings.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: With `OPENCLAW_LOAD_SHELL_ENV=1` or config-driven shell env import enabled on native Windows, OpenClaw should not try to execute `/bin/sh` and should not emit `[openclaw] shell env fallback failed: spawnSync /bin/sh ENOENT`.

Native Windows environment tested: Windows 10.0.26200 x64, Node `22.22.3`, checkout at `C:\src\openclaw-85739-win` on branch `fix-84795-win32-shell-env-fallback`, head `fde7096e11e8b21dc474d1f9ceac3ad330432e33`.

Exact affected command run after this patch:

```powershell
$env:OPENCLAW_LOAD_SHELL_ENV='1'
$env:OPENCLAW_HOME='C:\tmp\oc85739-home'
node .\oc85739-entry.mjs status
```

Evidence after fix: the command exited `0`, stderr was empty, stdout started with `OpenClaw status`, and a captured stdout scan for `/bin/sh|ENOENT` returned `False`. The status output reported `OS windows 10.0.26200 (x64) · node 22.22.3`, proving the smoke ran on native Windows rather than WSL.

Observed result after fix: native Windows status starts normally, the shell-env fallback does not attempt `/bin/sh`, and no ENOENT fallback warning is emitted. Direct module proof of `loadShellEnvFallback` on the same machine also returned `{ ok: true, applied: [] }` with no warnings.

What was not tested: I did not run the globally installed `openclaw` package. The smoke used a local bundle from this PR checkout because this Windows machine intermittently denies pnpm/esbuild postinstall child-process shims, while the native Windows entry bundle and Node runtime execute correctly.

Fixes #83925.

## Verification

2026-05-24 update after rebasing onto current `main`, head `2fd3cd10ca`:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/node-cli/daemon.test.ts --reporter=dot` -> 1 file, 2 tests passed
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md src/cli/node-cli/daemon.ts src/cli/node-cli/daemon.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/cli/node-cli/daemon.ts src/cli/node-cli/daemon.test.ts`
- `git diff --check main...HEAD`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `openclaw node status` printed follow-up recovery hints to stderr for loaded-but-stopped and missing service-unit states, unlike the unloaded branch which already printed hints on stdout.

Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu-24.04, source checkout at `/root/src/openclaw-85780`, branch `fix-83925-node-status-hints`, head `2fd3cd10ca`.

Proof setup: created a temporary unstarted systemd user node service from the patched checkout, using a disposable `OPENCLAW_HOME` and a temporary untracked `dist/entry.js` stub only for service installation. The service was uninstalled immediately after the status capture, and cleanup confirmed `openclaw-node.service` was `not-found` afterward.

```bash
OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/... OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 node --import tsx src/entry.ts node install --force
OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/... OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 node --import tsx src/entry.ts node stop
OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/... OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 node --import tsx src/entry.ts node status >status.stdout 2>status.stderr
OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/... OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 node --import tsx src/entry.ts node uninstall
systemctl --user is-enabled openclaw-node.service
```

Redacted output after fix:

```text
--- status stdout ---
Service: systemd user (enabled)
Command: /usr/bin/node /REDACTED/openclaw-85780/dist/entry.js node run --host 127.0.0.1 --port 18789

not-found

target.title: Fix Discord progress final and status command output
target.body:
## Summary

- omit `command-output` titles when channel progress is configured with `commandText: "status"`
- route Discord `onCommandOutput` progress through the same config-aware formatter as tool/item progress
- keep raw/default behavior unchanged so command-output titles still render outside status mode
- keep Discord `progress` previews temporary by clearing the live progress draft before sending the final reply normally
- clear/cancel pending and in-flight progress drafts even before Discord has assigned a draft message id
- suppress late Discord error finals after a visible final reply has already been queued/delivered, so a trailing tool warning cannot replace or follow the real answer
- add regression assertions for the shared formatter and Discord progress/final delivery behavior

## Why

`commandText: "status"` already suppresses command text for command/tool progress, but `command-output` lines still appended `input.title`. That can expose internal command titles in channel progress even when the channel is configured for status-only command display.

The shared formatter handled this correctly, but Discord's `onCommandOutput` callback was still calling the raw formatter directly. This branch now passes Discord command-output events through the config-aware formatter, matching the existing `onToolStart` and `onItemEvent` behavior.

A second Discord-specific failure remained after that formatter fix: in `progress` streaming mode the live tool-progress draft could be treated as the final reply container. If the turn hit failed tools, compaction, or fresh-thread pressure, Discord could leave the progress bubble visible as the last apparent assistant message instead of showing the final assistant reply. Progress previews should stay temporary; final replies should go through the normal message path.

There is also a pending-draft race: a final reply can arrive while the progress draft send is still pending or in flight and no Discord message id exists yet. Final cleanup must call the draft cleanup path anyway, because that is what cancels pending work and waits for in-flight sends before the normal final reply is delivered.

Finally, after the assistant has already queued/delivered a visible final reply, a subsequent tool-error final can still be queued as a second final. In Discord this can make the final visible event look like the tool failure instead of the answer. Once a non-error final reply is accepted, later error finals are suppressed; error finals still deliver when they are the only final outcome.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: A `command-output` progress event with `commandText: "status"` should render the status/exit code without appending the command-output title. Discord `progress` drafts should be cleared before the final reply is delivered normally, including the case where no draft message id exists yet, and late tool-error finals should not appear after a visible final reply.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on this PR branch, plus redacted live Discord gateway smoke runs using the built Discord extension from this branch. The final smoke used an isolated Discord gateway configured with `streaming.mode: "progress"`, `progress.toolProgress: true`, and `progress.commandText: "status"`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

1. Render a failed `command-output` event with default options and with `{ commandText: "status" }`.
2. Run the Discord message-handler regression suite covering config-aware command-output progress, progress draft final delivery, pending/no-id progress draft cleanup, and late error-final suppression.
3. Build/load the Discord extension from this PR into an isolated Discord gateway.
4. Configure the isolated gateway with `progress.commandText: "status"` and restart only that isolated gateway.
5. Send a live Discord prompt that forces one tool command equivalent to `printf '<redacted_marker>\n'; exit 42`, then returns a normal final assistant message.

Evidence after fix:

```console
$ node --import tsx - <<'JS'
import { formatChannelProgressDraftLine } from './src/plugin-sdk/channel-streaming.ts';
const input = {
  event: 'command-output',
  name: 'exec',
  exitCode: 1,
  title: 'run internal config check failed',
};
console.log('default:', formatChannelProgressDraftLine(input));
console.log('status:', formatChannelProgressDraftLine(input, { commandText: 'status' }));
JS
default: 🛠️ exit 1; run internal config check failed
status: 🛠️ exit 1
```

```console
$ pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-discord.config.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  77 passed (77)
```

```console
$ pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.plugin-sdk.config.ts src/plugin-sdk/channel-streaming.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  22 passed (22)
```

```text
Live Discord smoke, isolated gateway with commandText=status:
- the PR-built Discord extension was selected by explicit plugin config
- progress mode was active with tool progress enabled and command text set to status
- the assistant executed the requested tool command and the runtime recorded exitCode=42 with the redacted stdout marker
- Discord message history after the turn contained the normal final assistant reply marker as the latest assistant message
- no `exit 42` tool-error final replaced or followed the normal assistant final
```

Observed result after fix: Default/raw behavior still includes the title. Status mode renders only `exit 1`, so the command-output title is suppressed. Discord progress mode now uses that config-aware formatter for real `onCommandOutput` callbacks; the Discord regression asserts the progress draft contains `exit 1` and not the command-output title. Discord progress-mode final replies no longer edit the progress draft in place; tests assert the progress draft is cleared, pending/no-id progress drafts still call `clear()` before final delivery, `deliverDiscordReply` sends the final separately, and later tool-warning finals are suppressed after a visible final. The live Discord status-mode smoke matched the delivery behavior: a failed tool with exit code 42 was followed by the normal assistant final as the visible final message.

What was not tested: A Telegram transport smoke was not run for this PR. The shared formatter behavior is covered by plugin-sdk tests; the live transport proof above is Discord-specific.

## Test

- `pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.plugin-sdk.config.ts src/plugin-sdk/channel-streaming.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-discord.config.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.test.ts`

commands.author: 100yenadmin

target.title: [Feature]: Channel Broker Phase 4 constrained provider capabilities
target.body:
## Maintainer TL;DR This stack consolidates the recurring Telegram/Discord/Slack/WhatsApp/Signal/iMessage/etc. maintenance churn behind one Channel Broker contract. OpenClaw keeps the shared semantics that repeatedly regress across native channel plugins: sessions, allowlists, routing, /verbose, streaming policy, durable final sends, receipts, retries, and auditability. Broker providers own platform mechanics: bot APIs, bridge/device state, native ids, delivery quirks, and provider capabilities. This PR keeps WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and BlueBubbles honest by making hosted, device-bound, self-hosted, Mac-hosted, and bridge-backed constraints visible instead of pretending they are equivalent official bot APIs. The direction is intentionally incremental: first land the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, then prove conformance, then prove opt-in routing for the major channels, then model long-tail and constrained providers. Only after migration paths exist should legacy native-channel feature work freeze in #86115. ## Why This Is Better Today, one fix to streaming, /verbose, or delivery behavior can make Telegram fast this week and then break Discord, Slack, or another native plugin the next week. The broker path makes those shared semantics one OpenClaw-owned contract instead of many platform-specific re-implementations. When a provider adds support for previews, threads, receipts, media, or constrained account modes, that capability can become available through the same contract to routed channels whose provider declares and passes conformance for it. ## Stack Shape ```mermaid flowchart TD U["Umbrella issue #86105: reduce maintained-channel churn"] --> P1["#86096 Phase 1: SDK + bundled broker foundation"] P1 --> P2A["#86153 Phase 2A: conformance + signed inbound"] P2A --> P2B["#86154 Phase 2B: Telegram opt-in proof"] P2B --> P2C["#86156 Phase 2C: Discord opt-in proof"] P2C --> P2D["#86157 Phase 2D: Slack opt-in proof"] P2D --> P3["#86164 Phase 3: official/app capability matrix"] P3 --> P4["#86165 Phase 4: constrained provider capabilities"] P4 -. "future, not in this stack" .-> P5["Future freeze issue #86115: legacy native-plugin freeze"] ``` | PR | Role in the consolidation | | --- | --- | | #86096 | Creates the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, provider routing guard, media final-send mapping, SecretRef/cancellation handling, and prefix-ownership contract; carries a test-only upstream CI mock refresh. | | #86153 | Proves shared lifecycle conformance and adds signed inbound webhook registration/runtime handoff: inbound dedupe, durable final sends, receipts, retryable failures, preview/progress gates, and /verbose channelData. | | #86154 | Proves Telegram topics can route through broker targets without native Telegram internals. | | #86156 | Proves Discord DMs/channels/threads preserve conversation semantics through broker routing. | | #86157 | Proves Slack DMs/channel threads preserve direct/thread semantics through broker routing. | | #86164 | Adds official/app platform aliases, capability matrix rows, and declared delivery capability enforcement for long-tail channels. | | #86165 | Adds constrained-provider metadata for WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and bridge-backed providers. | ## Ownership Model ```mermaid flowchart LR Core["OpenClaw core
sessions, routing, allowlists
/verbose, streaming, retries
receipts, audit"] --> Broker["channel-broker plugin
openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-broker
V1 protocol"] Broker --> Provider["Provider-owned broker
HTTP/webhook transport
platform adapters"] Provider --> Platforms["Telegram, Discord, Slack
Teams, Matrix, WhatsApp
Signal, iMessage, long tail"] Provider --> Broker Broker --> Core ``` ## Review Shape These PRs are intentionally linear. target.title: [Feature]: Channel Broker Phase 3 official channel capability matrix
target.body:
## Maintainer TL;DR This stack consolidates the recurring Telegram/Discord/Slack/WhatsApp/Signal/iMessage/etc. maintenance churn behind one Channel Broker contract. OpenClaw keeps the shared semantics that repeatedly regress across native channel plugins: sessions, allowlists, routing, /verbose, streaming policy, durable final sends, receipts, retries, and auditability. Broker providers own platform mechanics: bot APIs, bridge/device state, native ids, delivery quirks, and provider capabilities. This PR prevents the long tail from becoming a new pile of native branches. It expresses official app/API platforms as broker aliases and capability rows first, and it now enforces declared delivery capabilities before outbound handoff. The direction is intentionally incremental: first land the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, then prove conformance, then prove opt-in routing for the major channels, then model long-tail and constrained providers. Only after migration paths exist should legacy native-channel feature work freeze in #86115. ## Why This Is Better Today, one fix to streaming, /verbose, or delivery behavior can make Telegram fast this week and then break Discord, Slack, or another native plugin the next week. The broker path makes those shared semantics one OpenClaw-owned contract instead of many platform-specific re-implementations. When a provider adds support for previews, threads, receipts, media, or constrained account modes, that capability can become available through the same contract to routed channels whose provider declares and passes conformance for it. ## Stack Shape ```mermaid flowchart TD U["Umbrella issue #86105: reduce maintained-channel churn"] --> P1["#86096 Phase 1: SDK + bundled broker foundation"] P1 --> P2A["#86153 Phase 2A: conformance + signed inbound"] P2A --> P2B["#86154 Phase 2B: Telegram opt-in proof"] P2B --> P2C["#86156 Phase 2C: Discord opt-in proof"] P2C --> P2D["#86157 Phase 2D: Slack opt-in proof"] P2D --> P3["#86164 Phase 3: official/app capability matrix"] P3 --> P4["#86165 Phase 4: constrained provider capabilities"] P4 -. "future, not in this stack" .-> P5["Future freeze issue #86115: legacy native-plugin freeze"] ``` | PR | Role in the consolidation | | --- | --- | | #86096 | Creates the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, provider routing guard, media final-send mapping, SecretRef/cancellation handling, and prefix-ownership contract; carries a test-only upstream CI mock refresh. | | #86153 | Proves shared lifecycle conformance and adds signed inbound webhook registration/runtime handoff: inbound dedupe, durable final sends, receipts, retryable failures, preview/progress gates, and /verbose channelData. | | #86154 | Proves Telegram topics can route through broker targets without native Telegram internals. | | #86156 | Proves Discord DMs/channels/threads preserve conversation semantics through broker routing. | | #86157 | Proves Slack DMs/channel threads preserve direct/thread semantics through broker routing. | | #86164 | Adds official/app platform aliases, capability matrix rows, and declared delivery capability enforcement for long-tail channels. | | #86165 | Adds constrained-provider metadata for WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and bridge-backed providers. | ## Ownership Model ```mermaid flowchart LR Core["OpenClaw core
sessions, routing, allowlists
/verbose, streaming, retries
receipts, audit"] --> Broker["channel-broker plugin
openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-broker
V1 protocol"] Broker --> Provider["Provider-owned broker
HTTP/webhook transport
platform adapters"] Provider --> Platforms["Telegram, Discord, Slack
Teams, Matrix, WhatsApp
Signal, iMessage, long tail"] Provider --> Broker Broker --> Core ``` ## Review Shape These PRs are intentionally linear. GitHub still shows `main` as the base for each PR so maintainers can land them one by one from fork branches; the branches themselves are stacked, and maintainers can review the focused adjacent diffs here: | PR | Focused adjacent diff | | --- | --- | | #86153 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-protocol...feat/channel-broker-conformance | | #86154 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-conformance...feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing | | #86156 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing...feat/channel-broker-discord-routing | | #86157 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-discord-routing...feat/channel-broker-slack-routing | | #86164 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-slack-routing...feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities | | #86165 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities...feat/channel-broker-constrained-platform-capabilities | ## This PR - Phase: Phase 3 - Official/app platform capability matrix - Role: Adds official/app platform aliases, capability matrix rows, and declared delivery capability enforcement for long-tail channels. - Head: `1ca3f60b2f` ## Current Review State This PR is live for maintainer review. Current head: `1ca3f60b2f`. Focused local validation after the latest stack hardening and upstream/main rebase ran from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`: - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts` - `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/http-routes.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts` - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-runtime-api-guardrails.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.contracts-plugin.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-support.config.ts src/agents/model-fallback.probe.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.sanitize-session-history.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.sanitize-session-history.policy.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.openai-tool-id-preservation.test.ts src/agents/runtime-plan/build.test.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-runtime-api-guardrails.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.ts src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts` - `pnpm exec oxlint --tsconfig extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json extensions/channel-broker/runtime-api.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/http-routes.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/http-routes.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/accounts.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/outbound.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/platforms.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/target.ts` - `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test` - `pnpm format:docs:check` - `pnpm lint:docs` - `pnpm docs:check-mdx` - `pnpm docs:check-links` - `pnpm docs:check-i18n-glossary` skipped locally because this worktree had no merge-base context; GitHub Actions remains the broad docs authority. - `git diff --check` Broad validation remains owned by GitHub Actions. ## Summary - Adds the Phase 3 official/app channel capability matrix for Channel Broker. - Keeps the work at the broker contract/catalog layer: no native channel migration defaults change in this PR. - Stacks on Phase 2D Slack proof (#86157), Phase 2C Discord proof (#86156), Phase 2B Telegram proof (#86154), Phase 2A conformance baseline (#86153), and Phase 1 broker foundation (#86096). Closes #86113 Related #86105 Related #86108 Supersedes closed PR #86144, which was closed by the repository active-PR cap rather than by an implementation failure. ## What changed - Adds canonical broker platform aliases while keeping `platform: string` open for provider-owned long-tail targets: - `teams` / `msteams` -> `microsoft-teams` - `googlechat` -> `google-chat` - `qq` -> `qqbot` - Aligns `channels.channel-broker.*.capabilities` config with the SDK capability shape: - `delivery` - `live` - `receive` - `native` - Adds focused proof for Microsoft Teams, Google Chat, Matrix, LINE, Feishu, QQ bot, Zalo, Mattermost, Nextcloud Talk, Twitch, IRC, Nostr, Tlon, and Synology Chat. - Documents the Phase 3 platform matrix and SDK capability shape. Focused diff for this stack layer: https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-slack-routing...feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities ## Real Behavior Proof - **Behavior or issue addressed:** Official/app channel aliases normalize through the broker catalog, and Phase 3 platforms route through provider-owned broker requests without enabling native migration defaults. - **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`, using the Channel Broker plugin and SDK from the stacked rollout branch on May 24, 2026. - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a `node --import tsx --input-type=module` terminal proof that called `channelBrokerPlugin.message.send.text` with `to: "broker:teams:19:meeting-channel"` and captured the provider request passed to the configured broker runtime. - **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output excerpt from that local command: ```json { "label": "teams-alias", "messageId": "native-teams-alias", "platform": "microsoft-teams", "conversation": { "id": "19:meeting-channel", "type": "channel" }, "requirements": { "text": true } } ``` - **Observed result after fix:** The live plugin path canonicalized the Teams alias to `microsoft-teams` and produced a provider-owned broker request with no platform-specific native extension branch. - **What was not tested:** Live Teams, Google Chat, Matrix, regional, or self-hosted provider traffic; this PR proves the broker catalog/schema/conformance layer and leaves broad validation to GitHub Actions. ## Validation Run locally from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`: - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_INCLUDE_FILE=/Volumes/LEXAR/Codex/channel-broker-vitest-include.json pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts` - `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts` - `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json` - `pnpm tsgo:core:test` - `git diff --check` - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.ts src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/accounts.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/platforms.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/target.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts docs/channels/channel-broker.md docs/plugins/sdk-channel-broker.md` - `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/channel-broker --format unix` - `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/channels/channel-broker.md docs/plugins/sdk-channel-broker.md` - `pnpm dlx --config.resolution-mode=highest markdownlint-cli2 --config config/markdownlint-cli2.jsonc docs/channels/channel-broker.md docs/plugins/sdk-channel-broker.md` Note: `pnpm exec markdownlint-cli2 ...` was unavailable in the local workspace, so the documented `pnpm dlx` path was used. It linted the docs glob cleanly. ## Review state - Live for maintainer review; no longer draft. - This PR does not move native routing defaults for any Phase 3 channel.

target.body:
## Maintainer TL;DR This stack consolidates the recurring Telegram/Discord/Slack/WhatsApp/Signal/iMessage/etc. maintenance churn behind one Channel Broker contract. OpenClaw keeps the shared semantics that repeatedly regress across native channel plugins: sessions, allowlists, routing, /verbose, streaming policy, durable final sends, receipts, retries, and auditability. Broker providers own platform mechanics: bot APIs, bridge/device state, native ids, delivery quirks, and provider capabilities. This PR proves Slack DMs and channel threads can route through broker-owned semantics while native Slack remains the default path. The direction is intentionally incremental: first land the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, then prove conformance, then prove opt-in routing for the major channels, then model long-tail and constrained providers. Only after migration paths exist should legacy native-channel feature work freeze in #86115. ## Why This Is Better Today, one fix to streaming, /verbose, or delivery behavior can make Telegram fast this week and then break Discord, Slack, or another native plugin the next week. The broker path makes those shared semantics one OpenClaw-owned contract instead of many platform-specific re-implementations. When a provider adds support for previews, threads, receipts, media, or constrained account modes, that capability can become available through the same contract to routed channels whose provider declares and passes conformance for it. ## Stack Shape ```mermaid flowchart TD U["Umbrella issue #86105: reduce maintained-channel churn"] --> P1["#86096 Phase 1: SDK + bundled broker foundation"] P1 --> P2A["#86153 Phase 2A: conformance + signed inbound"] P2A --> P2B["#86154 Phase 2B: Telegram opt-in proof"] P2B --> P2C["#86156 Phase 2C: Discord opt-in proof"] P2C --> P2D["#86157 Phase 2D: Slack opt-in proof"] P2D --> P3["#86164 Phase 3: official/app capability matrix"] P3 --> P4["#86165 Phase 4: constrained provider capabilities"] P4 -. "future, not in this stack" .-> P5["Future freeze issue #86115: legacy native-plugin freeze"] ``` | PR | Role in the consolidation | | --- | --- | | #86096 | Creates the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, provider routing guard, media final-send mapping, SecretRef/cancellation handling, and prefix-ownership contract; carries a test-only upstream CI mock refresh. | | #86153 | Proves shared lifecycle conformance and adds signed inbound webhook registration/runtime handoff: inbound dedupe, durable final sends, receipts, retryable failures, preview/progress gates, and /verbose channelData. | | #86154 | Proves Telegram topics can route through broker targets without native Telegram internals. | | #86156 | Proves Discord DMs/channels/threads preserve conversation semantics through broker routing. | | #86157 | Proves Slack DMs/channel threads preserve direct/thread semantics through broker routing. | | #86164 | Adds official/app platform aliases, capability matrix rows, and declared delivery capability enforcement for long-tail channels. | | #86165 | Adds constrained-provider metadata for WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and bridge-backed providers. | ## Ownership Model ```mermaid flowchart LR Core["OpenClaw core
sessions, routing, allowlists
/verbose, streaming, retries
receipts, audit"] --> Broker["channel-broker plugin
openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-broker
V1 protocol"] Broker --> Provider["Provider-owned broker
HTTP/webhook transport
platform adapters"] Provider --> Platforms["Telegram, Discord, Slack
Teams, Matrix, WhatsApp
Signal, iMessage, long tail"] Provider --> Broker Broker --> Core ``` ## Review Shape These PRs are intentionally linear. GitHub still shows `main` as the base for each PR so maintainers can land them one by one from fork branches; the branches themselves are stacked, and maintainers can review the focused adjacent diffs here: | PR | Focused adjacent diff | | --- | --- | | #86153 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-protocol...feat/channel-broker-conformance | | #86154 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-conformance...feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing | | #86156 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing...feat/channel-broker-discord-routing | | #86157 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-discord-routing...feat/channel-broker-slack-routing | | #86164 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-slack-routing...feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities | | #86165 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities...feat/channel-broker-constrained-platform-capabilities | ## This PR - Phase: Phase 2D - Slack opt-in broker routing proof - Role: Proves Slack DMs/channel threads preserve direct/thread semantics through broker routing. - Head: `27b7eca94c` ## Current Review State This PR is live for maintainer review. Current head: `27b7eca94c`. Focused local validation for this phase is documented below. Broad validation remains owned by GitHub Actions. Native channel defaults remain unchanged unless this phase explicitly says otherwise. ## Summary - Adds the Phase 2D Slack migration proof for the Channel Broker rollout. - Keeps native Slack default behavior unchanged; this only proves opt-in broker routing for Slack targets. - Stacks on Phase 2C Discord proof (#86156), Phase 2B Telegram proof (#86154), Phase 2A conformance baseline (#86153), and Phase 1 broker foundation (#86096). Closes #86112 Related #86108 Related #86105 Supersedes closed PR #86142, which was closed by the repository active-PR cap rather than by an implementation failure. ## What changed - Normalize broker-prefixed Slack DM targets such as `broker:slack:user:U12345678` into broker provider requests with `conversation.type = "direct"`. - Canonicalize Slack broker session routes to the resolved platform target, preserving direct-route semantics as `slack:direct%3A` and thread routes as `slack:?threadId=`. ## Real Behavior Proof - **Behavior or issue addressed:** Slack DM and threaded-channel targets can route through `channel-broker` while preserving direct/channel conversation semantics and Slack `threadTs` as broker `threadId`. - target.title: [Feature]: Channel Broker Phase 2C Discord migration proof
target.body:
## Maintainer TL;DR This stack consolidates the recurring Telegram/Discord/Slack/WhatsApp/Signal/iMessage/etc. maintenance churn behind one Channel Broker contract. OpenClaw keeps the shared semantics that repeatedly regress across native channel plugins: sessions, allowlists, routing, /verbose, streaming policy, durable final sends, receipts, retries, and auditability. Broker providers own platform mechanics: bot APIs, bridge/device state, native ids, delivery quirks, and provider capabilities. This PR proves Discord DMs, channels, and threads can route through broker-owned semantics while native Discord remains the default path. The direction is intentionally incremental: first land the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, then prove conformance, then prove opt-in routing for the major channels, then model long-tail and constrained providers. Only after migration paths exist should legacy native-channel feature work freeze in #86115. ## Why This Is Better Today, one fix to streaming, /verbose, or delivery behavior can make Telegram fast this week and then break Discord, Slack, or another native plugin the next week. The broker path makes those shared semantics one OpenClaw-owned contract instead of many platform-specific re-implementations. When a provider adds support for previews, threads, receipts, media, or constrained account modes, that capability can become available through the same contract to routed channels whose provider declares and passes conformance for it. ## Stack Shape ```mermaid flowchart TD U["Umbrella issue #86105: reduce maintained-channel churn"] --> P1["#86096 Phase 1: SDK + bundled broker foundation"] P1 --> P2A["#86153 Phase 2A: conformance + signed inbound"] P2A --> P2B["#86154 Phase 2B: Telegram opt-in proof"] P2B --> P2C["#86156 Phase 2C: Discord opt-in proof"] P2C --> P2D["#86157 Phase 2D: Slack opt-in proof"] P2D --> P3["#86164 Phase 3: official/app capability matrix"] P3 --> P4["#86165 Phase 4: constrained provider capabilities"] P4 -. "future, not in this stack" .-> P5["Future freeze issue #86115: legacy native-plugin freeze"] ``` | PR | Role in the consolidation | | --- | --- | | #86096 | Creates the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, provider routing guard, media final-send mapping, SecretRef/cancellation handling, and prefix-ownership contract; carries a test-only upstream CI mock refresh. | | #86153 | Proves shared lifecycle conformance and adds signed inbound webhook registration/runtime handoff: inbound dedupe, durable final sends, receipts, retryable failures, preview/progress gates, and /verbose channelData. | | #86154 | Proves Telegram topics can route through broker targets without native Telegram internals. | | #86156 | Proves Discord DMs/channels/threads preserve conversation semantics through broker routing. | | #86157 | Proves Slack DMs/channel threads preserve direct/thread semantics through broker routing. | | #86164 | Adds official/app platform aliases, capability matrix rows, and declared delivery capability enforcement for long-tail channels. | | #86165 | Adds constrained-provider metadata for WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and bridge-backed providers. | ## Ownership Model ```mermaid flowchart LR Core["OpenClaw core
sessions, routing, allowlists
/verbose, streaming, retries
receipts, audit"] --> Broker["channel-broker plugin
openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-broker
V1 protocol"] Broker --> Provider["Provider-owned broker
HTTP/webhook transport
platform adapters"] Provider --> Platforms["Telegram, Discord, Slack
Teams, Matrix, WhatsApp
Signal, iMessage, long tail"] Provider --> Broker Broker --> Core ``` ## Review Shape These PRs are intentionally linear. GitHub still shows `main` as the base for each PR so maintainers can land them one by one from fork branches; the branches themselves are stacked, and maintainers can review the focused adjacent diffs here: | PR | Focused adjacent diff | | --- | --- | | #86153 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-protocol...feat/channel-broker-conformance | | #86154 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-conformance...feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing | | #86156 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing...feat/channel-broker-discord-routing | | #86157 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-discord-routing...feat/channel-broker-slack-routing | | #86164 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-slack-routing...feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities | | #86165 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities...feat/channel-broker-constrained-platform-capabilities | ## This PR - Phase: Phase 2C - Discord opt-in broker routing proof - Role: Proves Discord DMs/channels/threads preserve conversation semantics through broker routing. - Head: `0029083969` ## Current Review State This PR is live for maintainer review. Current head: `0029083969`. Focused local validation for this phase is documented below. Broad validation remains owned by GitHub Actions. Native channel defaults remain unchanged unless this phase explicitly says otherwise. ## Summary - Adds the Phase 2C Discord broker routing proof on top of #86154, #86153, and #86096. - Normalizes broker-routed Discord DMs and channel threads without importing native Discord extension internals. - Preserves non-channel conversation type in canonical broker routes so Discord DMs remain direct routes. ## Rollout Context Closes #86111 Related #86108 Related #86105 Stacked on #86154 Stacked on #86153 Stacked on #86096 Supersedes closed PR #86139, which was closed by the repository active-PR cap rather than by an implementation failure. Native Discord defaults stay unchanged. Existing prefix ownership still keeps `discord:` owned by the native Discord plugin when installed; this PR proves the opt-in broker route through `broker:discord:...` and the broker alias path when native Discord is absent. For review, the focused stack diff is: https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing...feat/channel-broker-discord-routing ## Real Behavior Proof - **Behavior or issue addressed:** Discord DM and threaded-channel targets can route through `channel-broker` while preserving direct/channel conversation semantics and provider-owned ids. - **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`, using the Channel Broker plugin and SDK from the stacked rollout branch on May 24, 2026. - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a `node --import tsx --input-type=module` terminal proof that called `channelBrokerPlugin.message.send.text` with `to: "broker:discord:user:123456789012345678"` and captured the provider request passed to the configured broker runtime. - **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output excerpt from that local command: ```json { "label": "discord-dm", "messageId": "native-discord-dm", "platform": "discord", "conversation": { "id": "123456789012345678", "type": "direct" }, "requirements": { "text": true } } ``` - **Observed result after fix:** The live plugin path produced a direct Discord broker provider request rather than an ambiguous channel-looking target. - **What was not tested:** Live Discord API traffic and the full local suite; this PR stays scoped to opt-in broker routing proof. ### Behavior OpenClaw can represent Discord DMs and channel threads through `channel-broker` using stable broker semantics: provider platform `discord`, direct/channel conversation type, conversation id, and optional thread id. ### Environment - Local checkout: `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker` - Branch: `feat/channel-broker-discord-routing` - Head: `0029083969` (current Discord proof head) ### Steps 1. Sent a synthetic message to `broker:discord:user:123456789012345678`. 2. Verified the provider request used `platform: "discord"`, `conversation.type: "direct"`, and `conversation.id: "123456789012345678"`. 3. Sent a synthetic message to `broker:discord:channel:222222222222222222?threadId=333333333333333333`. 4. Verified the provider request used channel conversation id plus `threadId`. 5. Resolved the outbound session route for the Discord DM target. 6. Verified the route stayed direct and canonicalized to `discord:direct%3A123456789012345678`. ### Evidence - `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts` - 2 files passed, 13 tests passed. - `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json` - passed. - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/channel-broker/src/target.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts` - passed. - `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/channel-broker --format unix` - passed. - `git diff --check` - passed. ### Observed Result Discord DMs and threads now flow through broker target parsing and provider request semantics, not through core or native Discord extension branches. ### Not Tested - Live Discord API traffic; this PR is the opt-in broker migration proof, not a live provider connector. - Full local suite. Per `/Volumes/LEXAR` policy, broad validation is left to GitHub Actions. ## Current Stack Gate Context This branch is rebased on the current Phase 1 foundation head. Focused local validation is documented above or below; broad validation remains on GitHub Actions.
